mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or median 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for concurrent monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based aggregate demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative median 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 over baseline models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction exceeding 1.0% month-over-month during late-cycle expansion phases indicates supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of late-cycle contraction instances, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.0% year-over-year forces market expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for real wage growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Co-movement periods yield negative median 20-day forward returns, or the joint signal underperforms single-series benchmarks in out-of-sample directional accuracy tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue bases, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth periods show negative median 60-day forward returns, or the correlation between GDP growth and equity returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion exceeding 0.5% month-over-month during post-recession recovery phases signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of recovery-phase expansion instances, or the signal fails to maintain positive alpha after adjusting for sector rotation effects.
